stereotype: «ResourceAccess» — owns settlement_state head-state aggregate (Postgres CNPG, one row per settlement/customer, mutated in place under version + applied_mutation dedup ledger)
volatility: Settlement/Customer head-state aggregate — customer registration, merchant-gateway binding, per-cycle settlement outcome, revenue-share term, and delinquency posture.

ops.signature: registerCustomer(settlementId SettlementID, expectedVersion Version, profile CustomerProfile, idempotencyKey) → Version
ops.stereotype: pure write — head-state mutation, version guard + dedup-first idempotency
ops.note: Records a customer registration, opening the settlement aggregate.

fields.name: Version
fields.note: New head-state version after the mutation

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error — Conflict | NotFound | Transient | Infrastructure | ContractMisuse

ops.signature: bindGatewayLive(settlementId SettlementID, expectedVersion Version, binding GatewayBinding, idempotencyKey) → Version
ops.stereotype: pure write — head-state mutation, version guard + dedup-first idempotency
ops.note: Records that the merchant-gateway binding for the deployed app is live.

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error — Conflict | NotFound | Transient | Infrastructure | ContractMisuse

ops.signature: settleCycle(settlementId SettlementID, expectedVersion Version, cycle CycleID, outcome SettlementOutcome, idempotencyKey) → Version
ops.stereotype: pure write — head-state mutation, version guard + dedup-first idempotency
ops.note: Records the settlement outcome for a billing cycle; money movement is a separate ledger step sequenced by the Manager.

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error — Conflict | NotFound | Transient | Infrastructure | ContractMisuse

ops.signature: resettleCycle(settlementId SettlementID, expectedVersion Version, cycle CycleID, correction SettlementOutcome, idempotencyKey) → Version
ops.stereotype: pure write — head-state mutation, version guard + dedup-first idempotency
ops.note: Records a correction to a previously-settled cycle (e.g. after chargeback); the financial reversal is a separate append-only Revenue Ledger entry.

fields.name: Correction
fields.note: Corrected outcome (head-state corrected in place; financial reversal is a separate Revenue Ledger append)

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error — Conflict | NotFound | Transient | Infrastructure | ContractMisuse

ops.note: Returns the whole settlement head-state (registration, gateway binding, cycle outcomes, revenue-share term, delinquency); NotFound if no row.

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error — NotFound | Transient | Infrastructure | ContractMisuse

ops.note: Returns customers persistently delinquent for the delinquency sweep; cross-row SELECT on a delinquency predicate.

fields.name: ProjectId
fields.note: Optional platform scope; nil for all customers

fields.name: Customers
fields.note: SettlementID + delinquency facts for each persistently delinquent customer

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error — Transient | Infrastructure | ContractMisuse

dataContracts: SettlementOutcome = { Net int64; /* terms applied, currency */ }

Settlement = { ID SettlementID; Version Version; Customer CustomerProfile; GatewayBinding *GatewayBinding; RevenueShare RevenueShareTerm; Cycles map[CycleID]SettlementOutcome; Delinquency *DelinquencyState }
fwra.Error = { Kind: Conflict | NotFound | Transient | Infrastructure | ContractMisuse }
errorModel: framework-go/resourceaccess.Error — kinds: Conflict (optimistic-concurrency version mismatch), NotFound, Transient (retryable), Infrastructure (retryable), ContractMisuse (non-retryable). Write-verb discipline (version guard + dedup-first idempotency) is identical to projectStateAccess.
idempotency: Write verbs use version guard (expectedVersion) for cross-workflow ordering and applied_mutation dedup ledger (dedup-first) for activity-retry idempotency. idempotencyKey is a caller-supplied opaque key derived by the Manager as '${workflowId}:${activityId}'. Conflict → Manager workflow-level re-read→re-apply.

revisions.summary: Design-only contract. One of three head-state components split from the original monolithic projectStateAccess. Six ops (4 write + 2 read). Not implemented — settlementManager not yet built. Mirrors projectStateAccess head-state shape. Three open questions recorded (OQ-1 design-only; OQ-2 resettleCycle semantics; OQ-3 aggregate keying deferred to D-MST).
